ops.title: toon-go's json.Number encode path loses integer precision past 2^53; route ints to int64/big.Int
ops.body:
`github.com/toon-format/toon-go` (the Go TOON encoder wrapped by `ccx toon`) coerces `json.Number` through `float64` in `normalizeNumberString`, so integers past 2^53 silently corrupt (e.g. `123456789012345678` → `…680`). The spike missed this; round-trip tests caught it.

Fix in `internal/toon` (`numberScalar`): route an integer-valued `json.Number` to a native `int64` (when it fits) or `*big.Int` (when it doesn't) so toon-go's precise `FormatInt`/`big.Int.String` path runs; leave non-integers as `json.Number` (its float canonicalization is correct). Big ints past JS `MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` emit as QUOTED decimal strings — correct TOON-spec behavior, digits preserved exactly. `compactJSON` (the smart-fallback size comparator) must serialize `int64`/`*big.Int` too, or the two encodings describe different values.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]].
